_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: LOCAL evidence-finalize batch — San Marcos CISD (TX) school board: Miguel Arredondo (at-large, current), Sandra Sepulveda Lopez (Dist 3 / Secretary, current), Margie T. Villalpando (Dist 2 — now FORMER, term ended May 2026, seat filled by appointee Freddy Duran). Nonpartisan board, no published per-trustee roll calls; scored only on individually-attributable, cited evidence. Board-collective actions without named votes left null per the discipline rule.

profile.background: Juan Miguel Arredondo. SMCISD District 1 trustee 2015-2023 (then youngest elected official in Hays County history); re-elected unopposed to an At-Large seat in 2024 (May 4 2024 election canceled). President/CEO, United Way of Hays & Caldwell Counties (since Jan 2025); former chief of staff to Democratic TX state Rep. Erin Zwiener. Twice ran for San Marcos mayor (2020 runoff, 2024 — lost). Openly gay; the only openly gay member of the San Marcos school board.

miguel-arredondo.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (LOCAL tier). Openly gay LGBTQ-inclusion advocate (Texas Observer self-quotes: 'Not once has a family been in crisis because of transgender bathrooms'; opposes 'attacks on trans kids' / book bans) -> biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE, family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE. Backed 2024 VATRE tax-rate increase and reserve-draw deficit budget, framed shortfall as state underfunding -> economic_stewardship q2 FALSE. As 2020 mayoral candidate supported city 'cite and release' ordinance / criminal-justice reform, drew police-association opposition -> public_justice q1 FALSE; q0 left null (mixed: also said he supports first responders, no defund advocacy or affirmative pro-police-expansion record -> high bar not met). No individual evidence found on life, christian liberty, election integrity, refuse-state-overreach, border, self-defense -> null. 2021 DWI arrest (stepped down from a subcommittee). Religion: none stated. Progressive-coded; 0 conservative-aligned positions found.

0.note: Openly gay; came out publicly 2017, the only openly gay SMCISD trustee. Affirmatively advocates LGBTQ inclusion -> does not affirm marriage as one man/one woman.

2.note: Self-statement dismissing concern over transgender bathrooms ('Not once has a family been in crisis because of transgender bathrooms'); supports trans students -> does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Affirmatively promotes LGBTQ inclusion/support services in schools; opposes 'attacks on trans kids' -> contrary to opposing LGBTQ promotion in schools.

2.note: Frames anti-LGBTQ-content/book-removal efforts as unnecessary 'rhetoric' and opposes book bans -> does not oppose SOGI/gender-ideology content in K-12.
Backed the 2024 VATRE tax-rate increase and a reserve-draw deficit budget, opposing program cuts -> contrary to opposing deficit spending / fiscal restraint.

0.note: Mixed: said he supports first responders, but reform-leaning; no affirmative pro-police-expansion advocacy and no defund advocacy -> high bar not met, null.

1.note: As 2020 mayoral candidate supported the city 'cite and release' ordinance and criminal-justice reform, drew opposition from the police officers association -> soft-on-crime-coded.

profile.background: Sandra Lopez (Sandra Sepulveda Lopez). Current SMCISD District 3 trustee and Board Secretary; succeeded former trustee Mayra Mejia. Ran unopposed and was declared elected to a new term (through ~2029) when the May 2026 election was canceled.

sandra-sepulveda-lopez.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17 (full LOCAL research sweep performed). Current sitting trustee (Dist 3, Board Secretary) per official SMCISD board roster (2026-06-17). Ran unopposed; May 2026 election canceled. Nonpartisan board with no published per-trustee roll-call votes; thorough research found NO individually-attributable, citable position by Lopez on any rubric topic (no quotes, questionnaires, endorsements, or named votes). Board-collective actions during her tenure (Dec 2025 retention of 32 challenged library titles; rejection of SB 11 classroom prayer time; $88M deficit budget; VATRE) are not individually attributed to her, so all cells left null per the discipline rule. Religion/church: none stated.

set.office: School Board Trustee, District 2 (former — term ended May 2026)

profile.background: Margie T. Villalpando. Long-tenured SMCISD District 2 trustee (~2008-2026) and Board Secretary; former Hays County Clerk and City Secretary of Martindale; founding member of Centro Cultural Hispano de San Marcos. Did not seek re-election; term ended May 2026 and the seat was filled by appointment (Freddy Duran now holds District 2).

margie-t-villalpando.notes_set: ROSTER FIX 2026-06-17: marked FORMER — no longer on the board. Term ended May 2026, did not seek re-election, May 2 2026 election canceled (no filers), seat filled by appointee; official SMCISD roster now lists Freddy Duran as District 2 trustee. Long-serving (~2008-2026) incumbent. Nonpartisan board with no published per-trustee roll calls; her individual record is administrative/process-oriented (bond promotion, board training, a 2025 recusal on the Centro Cultural Hispano land sale as a conflicted founder). Board actions during her tenure (2021 mask mandate; 2026 SB 11 prayer rejection) were not individually attributed to her -> not scored. No individually-attributable, citable rubric position found -> all cells null. Religion/church: none stated.
